items.title: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
items.raw_content: OpenAI is getting serious about courting enterprise users. On Tuesday, the AI lab released a new set of capabilities for Codex, meant to expand the agentic tool’s uses in the workplace. Together with the new tools, the company released an internal report on how Codex is being used for knowledge work, finding its uses go […]

items.title: Anthropic has officially filed to go public
items.raw_content: After months of speculation about whether OpenAI or Anthropic would be first in their race to IPO, Anthropic on Monday reached a key milestone: filing to kick off the process with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The filing sets the stage for what's sure to be a massive IPO. As of its fundraise last […]

items.title: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos  to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
items.raw_content: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing, its security vulnerability program, and access to Mythos to 150 organizations across 15 countries — targeting critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications where a cyberattack could affect 100 million people.

items.title: Microsoft Build 2026: All the news about Windows, AI, RTX Spark and more
items.raw_content: Microsoft’s annual developer conference is kicking off on June 2nd in San Francisco with the keynote presentation streaming live at 12:30PM ET / 9:30AM PT, and we will be following along here with everything as it’s announced.  The Verge’s Tom Warren reports that we can expect to hear about new AI models and agentic OpenClaw-like […]

items.title: ZeroDrift raises $10M to protect AI models from themselves
items.raw_content: A new AI compliance service sits between AI models and end users to flag and replace any messages that might present a compliance problem.
items.summary: A new AI compliance service sits between AI models and end users to flag and replace any messages that might present a compliance problem.

items.title: Rocket engine startup Impulse raises $500 million to hire people, not AI
items.raw_content: Engineering physical systems still depends on human talent, according to Impulse Space president Eric Romo.

items.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80B to pay for AI buildout
items.raw_content: "The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the company’s available supply," Alphabet said in its statement.

items.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
items.raw_content: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.

items.title: Gemini’s new AI agent is about as good as Google’s demo
items.raw_content: Google's new "24/7" AI agent, Gemini Spark, can be shockingly good at doing things on your behalf. But I'm not sure it's worth the financial cost and potential privacy tradeoffs. The company gave me access to Spark last week. Google advertises Spark as an AI agent that can take on tasks and work on them […]

items.title: Meta’s own AI was exploited to hijack Instagram accounts
items.raw_content: Meta's AI support chatbot helped hackers hijack Instagram accounts, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a video shared on Telegram, a hacker shows how they could take over an account by asking Meta's chatbot to switch the email associated with someone else's profile and then reset the password. The issue, which Meta says has […]

items.title: Water access is now a risk factor in SpaceX’s IPO
items.raw_content: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.
items.summary: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.

items.title: This AI weather startup is out-forecasting government agencies
items.raw_content: WindBorne benefits from its unique combination of model-building and data collection. The company now has about 400 balloons in flight gathering sensor readings at any given time, launched from 15 sites around the globe. The advances in its current model come from improvements in how the data collected by the balloons is fed into the models.

items.title: Strava blames zero-code AI apps and scrapers as it tightens API access
items.raw_content: The popular fitness-tracking platform, Strava, is restricting access to its API as part of efforts to clamp down on AI scraping, as reported earlier by TechCrunch.
